clause_anchor.span: "Processing" means live hosting of raw individual records in the production database and does not include diagnostic review, metric creation, cache retention, format conversion, or support troubleshooting.

deviations.original_text: "Processing" means any operation performed on Personal Data, including collection, access, storage, hosting, organization, retrieval, transmission, analysis, deletion, and return.
deviations.mutated_text: "Processing" means live hosting of raw individual records in the production database and does not include diagnostic review, metric creation, cache retention, format conversion, or support troubleshooting.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor may study service records, build trend reports, tune automated systems, create commercial benchmarks, and enhance offerings for accounts other than Customer.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will process Personal Data only as necessary to provide the services to Customer, secure and support those services, or comply with applicable law. Vendor will not use Personal Data for Vendor's own analytics, benchmarking, product development, model training, advertising, or other independent business purposes.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may study service records, build trend reports, tune automated systems, create commercial benchmarks, and enhance offerings for accounts other than Customer.

clause_anchor.span: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files and transfer coordination. Operational copies retained for continuity, diagnostics, or legal administration may be kept for 365 days under internal retention standards.

deviations.original_text: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
deviations.mutated_text: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files and transfer coordination. Operational copies retained for continuity, diagnostics, or legal administration may be kept for 365 days under internal retention standards.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will refer individual inquiries to Customer within 5 business days when it can identify the account associated with the request.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will provide request intake details, account identifiers, data exports, correction logs, deletion status, portability files, opt-out status, and objection records within 10 business days after Customer's written request.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will refer individual inquiries to Customer within 5 business days when it can identify the account associated with the request.

clause_anchor.span: After annual assurance materials are delivered, no further control review is available for the same contract year unless Vendor agrees in writing.

deviations.original_text: On at least 30 days' notice, Customer or its independent assessor may audit or inspect controls relevant to Personal Data once per calendar year and one additional time after a Personal Data Breach, regulatory request, or documented good-faith cause.
deviations.mutated_text: After annual assurance materials are delivered, no further control review is available for the same contract year unless Vendor agrees in writing.

distractors.span: Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to security and privacy questionnaires within 10 business days, and written control mappings needed to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
distractors.why_benign: The audit canary says an annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance if follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide export files, transfer coordination, and deletion support for secure transition, return, or deletion.
distractors.why_benign: The transition canary says a 30-day termination assistance period is acceptable.

distractors.span: If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.
distractors.why_benign: A DPA precedence clause for personal-data processing is customary and client-protective.

missing_info.topic: Vendor obligation to maintain records of processing activities
missing_info.context_anchor: Customer must demonstrate processor accountability to its own regulators and relies on Vendor to maintain records of processing activities for the services.
